p.em: Rule: IP always points to the next byte to be fetched by the dispatcher.

g.text: Bytecode Array (Physical Memory Layout) | sizeof=10 Bytes
Pattern A: Single Byte Advancement (e.g., HALT, ADD)
Pattern B: Multi-Byte Operand Fetch (e.g., LDC <u16>)
Pattern C: Control Flow Jump (e.g., JMP <u16>)

div.p: Advancement Rule: IP += 1 The dispatcher consumes the opcode and immediately increments the pointer before execution.
Advancement Rule: IP += 3 Total stride = 1 (Opcode) + 2 (Immediate Operand). IP points to the opcode of the next instruction.
Absolute Jump: The IP is overwritten with the value decoded from the operands. New IP = (Operand[0] << 8) | Operand[1]
